one_liner: ASTS at $73.88 is below both EMAs with RSI 41.9 approaching oversold — the space-based broadband thesis is intact but the stock is in a downtrend; watch for Wyckoff support at $64.89 for a potential reversal setup.

price_action.intraday_story: ASTS sold off from $75.34 to a low of $71.77 before closing at $73.88. Below both EMAs on low volume — downtrend with no buyer urgency.

news_catalyst.headline: 3 Space Economy Stocks to Buy in July — ASTS Among Them
news_catalyst.summary: ASTS featured in a space economy buy list — the commercial satellite broadband thesis remains bullish but stock is technically in a downtrend below both EMAs. SpaceMobile Block 2 satellites are the key commercial milestone to watch.

technical.summary: ASTS below both EMAs (EMA50 $83.07, EMA200 $86.01) in a clear downtrend. RSI 41.9 approaching oversold. Wyckoff support at $63.93 is the next meaningful floor — 14% below current price. No DCF (pre-revenue). Wait for the support test.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Neutral Wyckoff with no signals. Monitoring $63.93 Wyckoff support for a potential Selling Climax/Spring setup.

valuation.summary: Pre-revenue satellite broadband — no DCF. Value depends on Block 2 satellite deployment and commercial service launch timeline.

key_strengths: Partnerships with major telecom carriers globally
key_concerns: Pre-revenue; massive capex needed for full constellation
fundamentals.summary: C grade speculative. Thesis is compelling long-term but current technical downtrend argues for patience.

risks: Break below EMA50 ($83) already happened — next support is Wyckoff $63.93
Commercial service launch delays would extend the pre-revenue period and pressure the stock
